# porthole

> Self-hosted localhost tunneling: expose your local server to the internet with a single CLI command.

porthole is an open-source alternative to ngrok-style tunnels. A persistent WebSocket connects your machine to a tunnel server; HTTP traffic for your subdomain is proxied to localhost.

## Install

- npm: `npm install -g @porthole-tunnel/cli`
- Homebrew: see repository `Formula/porthole.rb`
- GitHub: https://github.com/SaiBhargavRallapalli/porthole

## Quick start

```bash
porthole 3000
```

## Packages

- CLI (client): https://www.npmjs.com/package/@porthole-tunnel/cli
- Server (self-host): https://www.npmjs.com/package/@porthole-tunnel/server

## Documentation

- Repository README: https://github.com/SaiBhargavRallapalli/porthole/blob/main/README.md
- CLI README: https://github.com/SaiBhargavRallapalli/porthole/blob/main/packages/cli/README.md
- Server README: https://github.com/SaiBhargavRallapalli/porthole/blob/main/packages/server/README.md

## Site

- Landing page: https://porthole.devbench.co.in
